# Project instructions for coding agents

Project prefix: `ATLAS`.

## Baseline rules

- Treat canonical project documents and the installed consumer contract as the source of truth.
- Execute repository changes only from an accepted task brief with a valid base commit and path scope.
- Do not read or expose secrets, restricted documents, customer data or complete AI chat transcripts.
- Do not edit generated adapters, context packages or `docs/80-winzard` manually.
- Stop when a forbidden path, destructive operation, security-boundary change or missing human gate becomes necessary.
- Context access does not grant tool, merge or release permission.

## Source contracts

- ATLAS-POLICY-AI-001: AI-assisted Delivery Policy
- WZ-CONTRACT-DELIVERY-001: Winzard delivery contract
- WZ-CONTRACT-DOCUMENTATION-001: Winzard project documentation contract
- WZ-CONTRACT-GENERATED-001: Generated code and documentation policy
- WZ-CONTRACT-MODULES-001: Winzard module boundaries

---

## ATLAS-POLICY-AI-001 — AI-assisted Delivery Policy

Source: `docs/70-ai/policies/ATLAS-POLICY-AI-001.md`
Hash: `sha256:a3648a7332e8ce77567ffb4a5d6f3b3e2a576589af44cfb864dfaa983d0a1b1b`

## Contract

- AI execution requires an accepted task brief with a base commit, allowed paths and required checks.
- AI-generated ADRs and specifications remain proposed until human approval.
- Merge and release require an explicit integrator gate.
- Context access and tool permission are separate controls.

## Constraints and prohibitions

- Do not include secrets, customer data or complete chat transcripts in documentation or context packages.
- Do not modify generated adapters or consumer contracts manually.
- Do not continue when a forbidden path or destructive operation becomes necessary.

## Security requirements

- Missing approval enforcement fails closed.
- Restricted documents require explicit task-level access.
- The implementer cannot verify its own handoff as final evidence.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AI adapters are generated from accepted project contracts.
- [ ] Context packages are deterministic and provenance-tracked.
- [ ] Task, handoff and review remain separately attributable.

---

## WZ-CONTRACT-DELIVERY-001 — Winzard delivery contract

Source: `docs/80-winzard/platform-contracts/WZ-CONTRACT-DELIVERY-001.md`
Hash: `sha256:bd5d93d319380cf9c33b3759de0e38d0bde6ad52977a8f7cd43a587a96e65076`

## Contract

- Executable work starts from a task brief with a base commit, allowed paths, forbidden paths and required checks.
- Scope expansion, destructive changes and security-boundary changes require explicit approval.
- Implementers produce a structured handoff; a separate review verifies the result.
- Claims of completion are backed by named commands, exit results, commits or artifacts.
- Context access does not grant tool permission or merge authority.

## Acceptance criteria

- A task can be traced to its decisions, specifications, handoff, review and evidence.

---

## WZ-CONTRACT-DOCUMENTATION-001 — Winzard project documentation contract

Source: `docs/80-winzard/platform-contracts/WZ-CONTRACT-DOCUMENTATION-001.md`
Hash: `sha256:5cac8612bfb3cbb1dca821eab0fdc2c22c45d9a406c3f3316e2b88f3ef558eda`

## Contract

- The deployed project owns its product, domain, architecture, delivery and operations documentation.
- Canonical documents use Markdown, YAML frontmatter, stable project-prefixed IDs and Git history.
- Human and AI views are projections of the same canonical sources.
- Document, implementation and verification lifecycle states remain separate.
- Generated files under `docs/80-winzard` and `docs/90-generated` are read-only.
- Internal Winzard roadmap, tasks, handoffs, incidents and non-public ADRs must not be copied into a project.

## Constraints and prohibitions

- Do not use AI chat history, dashboards, Canvas files or generated indexes as the only source of truth.
- Do not include secrets, customer credentials or production payloads in documents or context packages.
- Do not edit generated consumer contracts or AI adapters directly.

## Acceptance criteria

- The project passes `forge docs:check`.
- Canonical IDs are unique and relationships resolve.
- Generated adapters and context packages match their source hashes.

---

## WZ-CONTRACT-GENERATED-001 — Generated code and documentation policy

Source: `docs/80-winzard/platform-contracts/WZ-CONTRACT-GENERATED-001.md`
Hash: `sha256:47938875e69ebfe9e03800f230ed122e647e753386b26e0a87c85c93cdd462db`

## Contract

- Generated outputs declare their generator and source contract version.
- Manual edits to generated outputs are drift and are rejected by checks.
- Fixes belong in canonical source documents or generators.
- Generated outputs must be deterministic for the same source set and generator version.

## Acceptance criteria

- `forge docs:adapters --check`, `forge context:check` and `forge docs:sync --check` detect drift.

---

## WZ-CONTRACT-MODULES-001 — Winzard module boundaries

Source: `docs/80-winzard/platform-contracts/WZ-CONTRACT-MODULES-001.md`
Hash: `sha256:8b20a96923ec03e3197f492e2f68a120de4a83e56da171be574171830c8a1faf`

## Contract

- `src/app` is a delivery adapter and does not own business logic or direct ORM access.
- Application code depends inward on domain contracts and ports.
- Infrastructure implements ports and may depend on runtime libraries.
- Composition roots wire concrete adapters and are server-only.
- Client Components do not import server-only, database, composition or infrastructure modules.

## Acceptance criteria

- `forge check` reports no architecture-boundary violations.
